title: Ampelocissus helferi
taxonomy: Ampelocissus helferi belongs to the family Vitaceae, commonly known as the grape family. This classification places it within the order Rosales, which includes other plant species known for their edible fruits and berries. At the kingdom level, Ampelocissus helferi is classified as Plantae, emphasizing its status as a plant species. Within the class of flowering plants, Ampelocissus helferi is classified as Magnoliopsida, indicating its complex floral structure. As a species, Ampelocissus helferi is further classified as a member of the genus Ampelocissus, which includes several other similar plant species.
uses: Ampelocissus helferi is known to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. Its extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's leaves and stems are used to make medicinal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which are consumed to alleviate symptoms. The plant's properties are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it a sought-after remedy in some parts of the world. preparations: Ampelocissus helferi, also known as the Himalayan grape, is a plant with various uses in traditional medicine. side_effects: Ampelocissus helferi is reported to be potentially toxic, as it contains saponins and other compounds that may cause adverse effects when ingested in large quantities. It may cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting, and can also lead to skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. The plant's toxicity can be exacerbated by its seeds, which are said to be particularly potent. Ingestion of Ampelocissus helferi should be avoided, especially by pregnant or breastfeeding women, as it may pose risks to the developing fetus or infant. intro: Ampelocissus helferi, also known as Helfer's grapevine, is a perennial climber native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ampelocissus helferi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, Ampelocissus helferi is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
